title: chenopodium ambrosioides proles suffruticosum
intro: Chenopodium ambrosioides proles suffruticosum, also known as Mexican tea or epazote, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the Chenopodium genus of plants. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and digestive-aiding properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing flatulence, alleviating menstrual cramps, and acting as an anthelmintic to remove intestinal parasites. Herbal preparations of Mexican tea can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
